text.title: REST API Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every api-rest-design artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Resource paths MUST use plural nouns: /orders, /users. Singular nouns and verbs (/getOrder, /createUser) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Consistency lets consumers predict URLs without reading docs; verbs in URLs duplicate HTTP method semantics.

rule.statement: GET / PUT / DELETE MUST be idempotent. POST / PATCH MUST be safe to call once but may not be idempotent without an Idempotency-Key header. Endpoints whose semantics contradict the verb (e.g. GET that mutates) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Caches, proxies, and retries assume HTTP semantics; violating idempotency turns retries into duplicated charges.

rule.statement: Status codes MUST follow RFC 9110: 200 success-with-body, 201 created (Location header), 204 success-no-body, 400 validation, 401 auth, 403 permission, 404 missing, 409 conflict, 422 semantic-invalid, 429 rate-limit, 5xx server. Repurposed codes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Consumers (and CDNs / browsers) rely on RFC-correct codes; repurposing breaks caching and retry behaviour.

rule.statement: POST endpoints that create monetary or external side-effects MUST accept an Idempotency-Key header AND cache the response for >= 24h. Replay returns the original response, never a duplicate charge.
rule.rationale: Network retries are unavoidable; without idempotency keys, retries become double-charges.

rule.statement: Resources with discoverable state transitions (e.g. order: pending → paid → shipped) MUST expose `_links` (or similar) with next-action URIs. Bare resources for stateful workflows are forbidden.

rationale.source: Richardson Maturity Model Level 3 + RFC 8288 Web Linking

rule.rationale: Without links, clients hard-code transition URLs; API evolution breaks them. Links make the workflow self-describing.
